_note: Worked example: Northbridge Capital Partners, a fictional institutional investment manager. Portfolio accounting, OMS/EMS trading, security master, custodian position feeds, private asset NAVs, risk/compliance, raw/staging/xref/core/warehouse layers, marts, control views, and DQ views are built as one deterministic SQLite ecosystem.

calendar._note: Capital-markets business-day shape: very low weekends, month/quarter-end close spikes, and muted August/December activity.

tables.name: application
tables.purpose: Application catalog for the investment management landscape.

tables.name: asset_class
tables.purpose: Investment asset-class taxonomy.

tables.name: custodian
tables.purpose: External custodians providing settled positions, cash, and transaction feeds.

tables.name: security
tables.purpose: Tradable public security and externally reported fund/security master.

tables.name: portfolio
tables.purpose: Portfolio/accounting book: pension pools, internal mandates, external mandates, and overlays.

tables.name: account
tables.purpose: Custodian/accounting account per portfolio and custodian relationship.

tables.name: trade_order
tables.purpose: OMS trade orders before execution/allocation.
tables.grain: one row per portfolio trade order

tables.name: order_event
tables.purpose: Order status history emitted by the OMS lifecycle machine.
tables.grain: one row per order state entered

tables.name: execution
tables.purpose: EMS fills/executions against OMS orders.

tables.name: raw_position
tables.purpose: Custodian settled position file rows before mapping and reconciliation.

tables.name: commitment
tables.purpose: Limited partner commitments by portfolio to private fund vehicles.

tables.name: nav_statement
tables.purpose: Quarterly manager NAV statements for private commitments.
tables.grain: one manager NAV statement per commitment period/version

tables.name: guideline_rule
tables.purpose: Investment guideline/compliance rule catalog.

tables.name: guideline_breach
tables.purpose: Guideline breach workflow items emitted by overnight monitoring.
tables.grain: one detected guideline breach per portfolio/rule/date

tables.name: guideline_breach_event
tables.purpose: Guideline breach workflow status history.
tables.grain: one row per breach state entered

tables.name: file_load
tables.purpose: Batch/API feed load log for custodian, security, price, and manager files.

tables.name: recon_run
tables.purpose: Control execution log for portfolio/accounting reconciliations.

tables.name: rule
tables.purpose: Data-quality rule catalog for the current-result view.

tables.name: custodian_position_extract
tables.purpose: Raw landed custodian position extract with source-formatted dates.

tables.name: custodian_position
tables.purpose: Parsed/staged custodian positions with nullable parse results.

tables.name: security_identifier_map
tables.purpose: Crosswalk from custodian security ids to internal security master ids.

tables.name: holding_position
tables.purpose: Canonical IBOR/custodian holding position rows after security mapping.

tables.name: dim_portfolio
tables.purpose: Portfolio dimension from the portfolio accounting master.

tables.name: dim_security
tables.purpose: Security dimension from the security master.

tables.name: fact_trade_allocation
tables.purpose: Trade allocation fact sourced from OMS/EMS executions.

tables.name: fact_holding_daily
tables.purpose: Daily holding-position fact derived from custodian positions.

derivations.name: raw_custodian_position_extract
sql: insert into raw.custodian_position_extract

select cast(raw_position_id as text), cast(account_id as text), portfolio_id, custodian_id, custodian_security_id, security_id, strftime('%m/%d/%Y', position_date), quantity, market_value, feed_file_name from custodian.raw_position;

derivations.name: stg_custodian_position
sql: insert into stg.custodian_position
select cast(raw_position_ref as integer), cast(account_ref as integer), portfolio_id, custodian_id, custodian_security_id, security_id,
case when source_position_date like '__/__/____' then substr(source_position_date,7,4) || '-' || substr(source_position_date,1,2) || '-' || substr(source_position_date,4,2) else null end,
quantity, market_value, case when source_position_date like '__/__/____' then 'parsed' else 'date_parse_error' end
from raw.custodian_position_extract;

derivations.name: xref_security_identifier_map
sql: insert into xref.security_identifier_map

select row_number() over (order by security_id), 'CustoLink', custodian_security_id, min(security_id), min(position_date), null, 1 from stg.custodian_position where position_date is not null group by custodian_security_id;

derivations.name: core_holding_position
sql: insert into core.holding_position
select s.raw_position_id, s.portfolio_id, s.account_id, x.security_id, s.position_date, s.quantity, s.market_value, case when x.security_id is not null then 1 else 0 end
from stg.custodian_position s left join xref.security_identifier_map x on x.source_security_id = s.custodian_security_id and x.active_flag = 1 where s.position_date is not null;

derivations.name: wh_dimensions
sql: insert into wh.dim_portfolio select portfolio_id, portfolio_code, portfolio_name, portfolio_type, asset_class_bias, status, aum_tier from portfolio.portfolio;
insert into wh.dim_security select security_id, security_uid, security_name, asset_class_code, currency, active_flag from sec.security;

derivations.name: wh_fact_trade_allocation
sql: insert into wh.fact_trade_allocation
select execution_id, execution_date, portfolio_id, security_id, side, fill_qty, fill_price, case when side = 'BUY' then gross_amount else -gross_amount end from trading.execution;

derivations.name: wh_fact_holding_daily
sql: insert into wh.fact_holding_daily

select h.holding_position_id, h.position_date, h.portfolio_id, h.security_id, coalesce(s.asset_class_code, 'UNMAPPED'), h.quantity, h.market_value, h.mapped_flag from core.holding_position h left join sec.security s on s.security_id = h.security_id;

derivations.name: mart_aum_by_asset_class
sql: create view mart_aum_by_asset_class as
select position_date, asset_class_code, count(distinct portfolio_id) as portfolios, count(*) as position_rows, round(sum(market_value), 2) as market_value from wh.fact_holding_daily group by 1, 2;

derivations.name: mart_private_nav_rollforward
sql: create view mart_private_nav_rollforward as

select c.strategy, count(distinct c.commitment_id) as commitments, round(sum(c.commitment_amount),2) as committed, round(sum(n.nav_amount),2) as reported_nav from private.commitment c left join private.nav_statement n on n.commitment_id = c.commitment_id group by 1;

derivations.name: mart_trade_settlement_funnel
sql: create view mart_trade_settlement_funnel as
select status, count(*) as orders, round(sum(notional_amount),2) as notional from trading.trade_order group by 1;

derivations.name: control_recon_custodian_vs_ibor
sql: create view control_recon_custodian_vs_ibor as
with c as (select portfolio_id, security_id, position_date, round(sum(market_value),2) as custodian_mv from core.holding_position group by 1,2,3),
t as (select portfolio_id, security_id, allocation_date as position_date, round(sum(net_amount),2) as ibor_trade_mv from wh.fact_trade_allocation group by 1,2,3)
select c.portfolio_id, c.security_id, c.position_date, c.custodian_mv, coalesce(t.ibor_trade_mv,0) as ibor_trade_mv, round(c.custodian_mv - coalesce(t.ibor_trade_mv,0),2) as break_amount

from c left join t on t.portfolio_id = c.portfolio_id and t.security_id = c.security_id and t.position_date = c.position_date where abs(c.custodian_mv - coalesce(t.ibor_trade_mv,0)) > 1000000 limit 5000;

derivations.name: control_private_nav_restatement
sql: create view control_private_nav_restatement as
select commitment_id, valuation_date, count(*) as versions, round(sum(nav_amount),2) as net_nav_versions from private.nav_statement group by 1,2 having count(*) > 1;

derivations.name: dq_rule_result_current
sql: create view dq_rule_result_current as

select 'DQ-001' as rule_code, 'duplicate security names' as issue, cast(security_id as text) as entity_id from sec.security s where exists (select 1 from sec.security s2 where s2.security_name = s.security_name and s2.security_id <> s.security_id)

union all select 'DQ-002', 'custodian position security not in security master', cast(raw_position_id as text) from custodian.raw_position rp where not exists (select 1 from sec.security s where s.security_id = rp.security_id)
union all select 'DQ-003', 'unparseable custodian position date', raw_position_ref from raw.custodian_position_extract where source_position_date not like '__/__/____'

union all select 'DQ-004', 'active security mapping past valid_to', cast(security_map_id as text) from xref.security_identifier_map where active_flag = 1 and valid_to is not null and valid_to < '2025-12-31'
union all select 'DQ-005', 'duplicate feed load file', file_name from integration.file_load group by file_name having count(*) > 1
union all select 'DQ-006', 'manual guideline waiver', cast(breach_id as text) from risk.guideline_breach where waiver_flag = 1

union all select 'DQ-007', 'staged custodian security missing map', cast(raw_position_id as text) from stg.custodian_position s where not exists (select 1 from xref.security_identifier_map x where x.source_security_id = s.custodian_security_id and x.active_flag = 1)
union all select 'DQ-008', 'trade order blocked by compliance', cast(order_id as text) from trading.trade_order where status = 'compliance_blocked'
union all select 'DQ-009', 'NAV restatement reason present', cast(nav_statement_id as text) from private.nav_statement where restatement_reason is not null;

imperfections.name: security_duplicate_setup
imperfections.scenario: Vendor and custodian identifiers create near-duplicate securities; DQ-001 catches the duplicate-name population.

imperfections.name: security_name_typos
imperfections.scenario: Manual security setup typo before steward review.

imperfections.name: custodian_security_orphans
imperfections.scenario: Custodian sends a position for an unapproved security id; DQ-002 and DQ-007 catch it.

imperfections.name: feed_late_arrivals
imperfections.scenario: Custodian or market-data files arrive after the warehouse cutoff.

imperfections.name: manual_guideline_waivers
imperfections.scenario: CIO-approved waivers are visible in DQ-006.

imperfections.name: file_load_duplicates
imperfections.scenario: SFTP resend lands the same file twice; DQ-005 dedupe queue catches it.

imperfections.name: raw_position_date_drift
imperfections.scenario: Custodian changes date padding; staging cannot parse the padded rows.

imperfections.name: missing_security_maps
imperfections.scenario: Security crosswalk rows missing after onboarding a new custodian.

imperfections.name: stale_security_maps
imperfections.scenario: Expired custodian identifier mappings remain active.

imperfections.name: private_nav_restatements
imperfections.scenario: Quarterly manager NAV corrections create reversal/restated versions and valuation review work.

controls.rule_name: Custodian market value reconciles to IBOR trade/holding view
controls.expected_break_rate: timing and mapping breaks visible in control_recon_custodian_vs_ibor

dq_rules.condition: security names should not duplicate across active ids
raw positions reference a known security
source position date parses as MM/DD/YYYY
staged securities have active crosswalk mappings
blocked trades remain visible for compliance review

dataflows.name: custodian_position_recon
dataflows.sequence: custodian.raw_position -> raw extract -> stg -> xref -> core.holding_position -> wh.fact_holding_daily -> control_recon_custodian_vs_ibor

dataflows.name: security_mastering
dataflows.sequence: sec.security + custodian security ids -> xref.security_identifier_map -> mapped core holdings
